tokens.range: a tent (as clearly conspicuous from a distance)
properly, to turn aside from the road (for a lodging or any other purpose), i.e. sojourn (as a guest); also to shrink, fear (as in a strange place); also to gather forhostility (as afraid)
to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in
an edge or extremity; specifically (of a bird or army) a wing, (of a garment or bedclothing) a flap, (of the earth) a quarter, (of a building) a pinnacle
suspension (of music), i.e. pause
a cover (in a good or a bad, a literal or a figurative sense)
properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always

flags.note: עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

4.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: hating; an adversary
a tower (from its size or height); by analogy, a rostrum; figuratively, a (pyramidal) bed of flowers

tokens.text: For thou hast been a shelter
tokens.range: a shelter (literally or figuratively)
strength in various applications (force, security, majesty, praise)
the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)

gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
fearing; morally, reverent
something occupied; a conquest; also a patrimony
a promise (to God); also (concretely) a thing promised
to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character
to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

stability; (figuratively) certainty, truth, trustworthiness
kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty
properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
properly, to weigh out; by implication, to allot or constitute officially; also to enumerate or enroll
to guard, in a good sense (to protect, maintain, obey, etc.) or a bad one (to conceal, etc.)

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

to be high actively, to rise or raise (in various applications, literally or figuratively)
